request: Decide whether the VIP Lake Travis 'Weekdays on the Water' landing concept is ready to send to the client.

inputs.note: Page '1E1E1E'; three frames: 'Desktop 1440 — Summer' (3 boat cards) and two unnamed desktop variants (2 boat cards).

constraints.brand: navy + coral + aqua on lake imagery

board.findings: Strengths worth protecting: a clear single-promise hero ('Your Best Summer Day Is One Click Away'), a legible booking funnel (value strip -> boat picker with prices -> social proof -> FAQ -> closing CTA), and a consistent 15% Weekday/Thursday hook. Weaknesses by area: HERO carries a rating card that competes with the primary CTA; HIERARCHY is even-weighted so nothing pulls the eye to 'book'; MOAT (why VIP vs. any rental) is never stated; PROOF section uses placeholder testimonial text; MOBILE fold does not exist. Punch-list: name one final frame; make the reserve CTA the single loudest element in the hero; add a one-line 'why VIP' under the H1; replace filler testimonials with real or clearly-labeled placeholders; standardize boat card count and naming.
evidence: Zoomed captures of all three frames at ~9% canvas zoom on page 1E1E1E

board.findings: Sharper POV without breaking the premium/honesty bar: lead the hero with loss aversion — 'Summer is 12 weekends. Don't spend one on the dock.' — and make the boat picker the hero instead of a mid-page section, so the first scroll is 'pick your boat + see the price.' Hold the line: no fabricated urgency counters, no invented review counts; the 15% offer stays the only scarcity lever because it is real.
evidence: Hero and boat-card structure observed in Frame 1 and Frame 2

board.findings: Lowest-objection version a cautious marina owner could not reject: keep the current calm structure, lead with trust (local, well-maintained fleet, easy booking), state prices plainly, and keep the closing reservation CTA. This is the safest path and is close to Frame 1 today; risk is that 'safe' stays generic unless the 'why VIP' line and real proof are added.
evidence: Frame 1 'Lake days should feel easy' value strip and FAQ block

board.findings: Exact requested format (Figma) is present and editable — pass. But required states fail: three desktop frames, no mobile/tablet. Cross-frame inconsistency: Frame 1 lists three boats (Tritoon $435 / Pontoon $335 / Sea-Doo $330); Frames 2-3 list two (Berkshire Tritoon $435 / Sea-Doo GTI 130 $330). No single frame is designated final. Testimonial copy is unverified filler.
evidence: Pricing and boat names read from zoomed captures of each frame

board.findings: Can the client decide from this without a walkthrough? Partially. They can react to the desktop look, but cannot approve a build: there is no phone view, and they would not know which of three frames is 'the' design or which boat lineup is real. Ask them exactly two things: which lineup/pricing is correct, and whether mobile is in scope this round.
evidence: Two-boat vs three-boat divergence across frames; absence of mobile frame

board.findings: Do first (ranked): 1) pick one canonical desktop frame; 2) confirm boat lineup + pricing with the client; 3) design the matching mobile frame; 4) replace placeholder testimonials. Highest-leverage single move: collapse to one final desktop frame — it unblocks mobile, copy, and client sign-off at once. Verdict: caution — strong, near-ready concept, but a required state is missing and there is no single source-of-truth frame.

evidence.statement: Native Figma design file, logged in, page '1E1E1E', three desktop frames.
Frame 1 = 3 boat cards (Tritoon $435 / Pontoon $335 / Sea-Doo $330); Frames 2-3 = 2 boat cards (Berkshire Tritoon $435 / Sea-Doo GTI 130 $330).
No mobile or tablet frame exists on the reviewed page.
Layer/component editability and client share-link permissions inspected visually only, not opened in the layers panel.

scorecard.evidence: Native, editable Figma file (not an export)
Visibly native; layer structure not opened
All frames render cleanly at desktop dimensions
Navy/coral/aqua on lake imagery; consistent 15% hook
Open locally; client share link not verified
Two of three frames unnamed; no final designated

decisionsNeeded: Boat lineup + pricing: 2-boat or 3-boat, prices approved?
Is mobile in scope this round, or desktop-only?
Real testimonials available, or approve labeled placeholders?

pathToShip.item: Consolidate to one named canonical desktop frame; drop duplicates
Draft real testimonial copy options to replace placeholders
Confirm mobile scope; if in scope, build the mobile frame from the spec

verdict.reason: Strong desktop concept, but no mobile state exists and the three frames disagree on the boat lineup, so there is no single buildable source of truth.
verdict.requiredProof: One named final desktop frame, a matching mobile frame, confirmed boat lineup + pricing, and real (or labeled) testimonial copy.
verdict.nextAction: Collapse to one final desktop frame, confirm lineup/pricing with the client, build the mobile frame, then re-run revüe targeting 'ship with changes'.
